response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior full-stack developer with strong testing culture and open-source contributions, but he does not meet the role's core minimum qualifications. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly a year (a 7-month stint at National Instruments plus a thesis project), far short of the required 8 years; his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/product work. Large-scale infrastructure experience is limited to under two years of low-latency microservices at Sociomantic, and he shows no sustained cloud compute platform ownership or formal technical-leadership track record mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers.
strengths: Broad, 15+ year engineering career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Genuine high-throughput systems exposure (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong software craftsmanship and mentoring signals: TDD, workshops, courses, and open-source test tooling
concerns: C++ experience (~1 year) falls drastically short of the 8-year minimum requirement
No sustained large-scale infrastructure or cloud compute platform experience (~2 years vs. 5 required)
No formal technical-lead role; leadership evidence is workshops and side projects rather than owning team strategy and delivery

key_factors.factor: Limited large-scale infrastructure and cloud platform tenure
